title: How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: French environment minister backtracks on resignation over 'urgency' to act on climate
Orléans museum launches 'Wanted' campaign to track down 424 looted artworks
Zelensky forced to dismiss Oleksandr Syrsky, Ukraine's armed forces chief
subhead: Yielding to mounting pressure from demonstrators calling for General Syrsky's departure, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky reversed course and has replaced him with General Mykhailo Drapaty.

title: Cédric Jubillar admitted to killing his wife after five years of denial. Here is what he told the authorities
subhead: 'It took me a few moments to realize I had just killed the mother of my children,' Cédric Jubillar said on July 15, according to an official court record Le Monde has read.

title: Louvre heist gallery reopens to public, but with nothing to steal
subhead: The Louvre Museum's Apollo Gallery reopened for the first time since thieves broke into it and stole jewels in October 2025. It has been emptied of the remaining jewels, which are set to be displayed in a secure space in the museum.

title: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.

Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhead: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
